mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production indicate expanding manufacturing and output capacity, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC of INDPRO with 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03, or the signal produces non-positive average returns in periods of sustained INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a critical threshold of 6.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate pricing power, thereby yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In sub-6% UNRATE periods, forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated housing starts and very low federal funds rates signal that accommodative monetary policy is effectively stimulating construction activity and durable goods demand, which jointly lift equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both HOUST > 1000 and FEDFUNDS < 1.0 are true, the average forward 20-day return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPI YoY growth above 4%), a positive CPI surprise erodes real purchasing power and forces the central bank to tighten policy, which depresses equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: During high inflation episodes, forward 20-day returns are not systematically negative (average return > 0 or win rate < 55%).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales indicate robust consumer demand, which directly feeds into higher corporate revenues and earnings, pushing the S&P 500 higher over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Rank IC of RSAFS with 5-day forward returns is below 0.03, or the average return in top-quintile RSAFS months is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, markets anticipate tighter monetary policy that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When CPI YoY exceeds 3%, the 5-day forward return is not consistently negative (average return < 0 in less than 60% of events).

mechanism_narrative: Strong simultaneous expansions in both GDP (demand) and industrial production (supply) indicate a balanced and sustainable economic upswing, which supports broad-based earnings growth and lifts equity prices over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 qoq growth > 2.5% and INDPRO mom growth > 0%
failure_condition: When both GDP and INDPRO growth conditions are met, the average 60-day forward return is not positive or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of elevated labor market slack (unemployment above 6%), a decline in the unemployment rate corresponds to increasing employment and income, which fuels consumption and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE > 6%, month-over-month declines in UNRATE do not produce average forward 20-day returns significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).
